original_abstract: Background: Fruit from the *Hylocereus polyrhizus* and *Hylocereus undatus* species, collectively known as pitaya or dragon fruit, has seen a rapid surge in global popularity. Despite its widespread consumption, comprehensive data regarding the chemical profiles and nutritional properties of both the flesh and the peel remain notably scarce. This study aims to address these gaps by evaluating the bioactive composition and nutritional quality of these fruit components.
humanized_abstract: Abstract Background Pitaya or dragon fruit derived from Hylocereus polyrhizus and Hylocereus undatus varieties has recently become one of the most frequently consumed exotic fruits worldwide. To date, there is a scarcity of studies on the detailed analysis of chemical composition and nutritional value of pitaya fruit pulp and rind. Thus, the present investigation was initiated with the aim of determining the bioactive compounds and assessing the nutritional content of the pitaya fruit components.

document.output: Abstract Background Pitaya or dragon fruit derived from Hylocereus polyrhizus and Hylocereus undatus varieties has recently become one of the most frequently consumed exotic fruits worldwide.
